name: Extra-Virgin Olive Oil

book_claims.claim: Suitable fat source during fasting days that does not disrupt fasting metabolic benefits
book_claims.mechanism: Provides essential fatty acids without activating insulin/IGF-1/mTOR pathway as animal proteins would

book_claims.claim: Slows absorption of carbohydrates when added to meals
book_claims.mechanism: Fat delays gastric emptying and carbohydrate absorption in the intestine

book_claims.claim: Monounsaturated fat from plant origins associated with lower cardiovascular and cancer death rate
book_claims.mechanism: Contains primarily monounsaturated fatty acids that do not increase cholesterol; reduces LDL without affecting HDL; reduces blood sugar and triglycerides in diabetes
book_claims.study_ref: Refs 66, 67, 68, 69 in book (Nurses' Health Study and others)

book_claims.claim: Excellent source of vitamin E and precious phytochemicals
book_claims.mechanism: Cold-pressed extraction preserves vitamin E (antioxidant) and polyphenols with anti-inflammatory properties

book_claims.claim: Plant-origin monounsaturated fats reduce coronary heart disease risk
book_claims.mechanism: Substituting monounsaturated fat for saturated fat reduces LDL-cholesterol without reducing HDL-cholesterol

book_claims.claim: Provides monounsaturated fatty acids that reduce LDL-cholesterol and heart disease risk
book_claims.mechanism: Replaces saturated fat intake; monounsaturated fat substitution for 5% of saturated fat calories associated with 15% lower coronary heart disease risk
Oleocanthal phytochemical inhibits inflammatory pathways, similar mechanism to ibuprofen

book_claims.claim: Reduces circulating oxidised LDL and inflammatory markers
book_claims.mechanism: Antioxidant compounds prevent oxidation of LDL particles, reducing binding to endothelial and immune cell receptors that drive atherosclerotic plaque growth

book_claims.claim: Mediterranean diet supplemented with EVOO reduces cardiovascular events, especially strokes
book_claims.mechanism: Combined anti-inflammatory, cholesterol-lowering and antioxidant effects reduce atherosclerosis progression

book_claims.claim: May reduce risk of type 2 diabetes, peripheral artery disease, atrial fibrillation and breast cancer
book_claims.mechanism: Multiple pathways including reduced inflammation and oxidative stress

book_claims.claim: Reduces plasma triglycerides, oxidative stress markers, and increases HDL-cholesterol. Phenolics inhibit inflammation and thrombosis.
book_claims.mechanism: Contains 30+ phytocompounds including oleuropein, tyrosol, hydroxytyrosol. 100g provides up to 25mg alpha-tocopherol, 20-500mg oleuropein, 98-185mg phytosterols.

book_claims.claim: Contains oleocanthal that prevents blood clots. 50g daily equivalent to a baby aspirin for heart disease prevention.
book_claims.mechanism: Oleocanthal has structural profile similar to ibuprofen. Anti-inflammatory and anti-platelet aggregation. The pungent, peppery throat sensation indicates oleocanthal presence.

supplementary_research.finding: Guasch-Ferre et al. (2022) in the Journal of the American College of Cardiology: prospective cohort study of 92,383 participants over 28 years found that consuming more than 0.5 tablespoon/day of olive oil was associated with 19% lower cardiovascular disease mortality, 17% lower cancer mortality, 29% lower neurodegenerative disease mortality, and 18% lower respiratory disease mortality compared with those who rarely consumed olive oil.

Estruch et al. (2018) republished PREDIMED results in NEJM: Mediterranean diet supplemented with EVOO reduced major cardiovascular events by approximately 30% compared with a reduced-fat control diet in a primary prevention trial of 7,447 participants at high cardiovascular risk.

Examine's systematic review of olive oil research confirms strong evidence for reduced LDL oxidation, anti-inflammatory effects via oleocanthal (COX-1 and COX-2 inhibition), improved endothelial function, and blood pressure reduction. Notes that benefits are dose-dependent and specific to high-polyphenol EVOO, not refined olive oil.

Schwingshackl et al. (2020) meta-analysis in Advances in Nutrition: dose-response analysis of olive oil intake and risk of CVD, cancer, and all-cause mortality. Each 25g/day increase in olive oil consumption was associated with 13% lower risk of all-cause mortality and 16% lower cardiovascular mortality.

Beauchamp et al. reported in Nature (2005, PMID 16136122) that oleocanthal is a natural anti-inflammatory agent inhibiting both COX-1 and COX-2 with potency comparable to ibuprofen. Confirmed by Scotece et al. (2015) showing oleocanthal suppresses MIP-1alpha and IL-6 in macrophages.

culinary_pairings.source: Foundational soffritto base across Southern European cuisines
Traditional hummus, pasta e fagioli, ribollita
Classic Italian salad dressing and drizzle

seasonality: Year-round availability. Freshest oil from November-February harvest in Northern Hemisphere (October-January in Southern Hemisphere). Best consumed within 12-14 months of harvest date.

nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: Highly bioavailable; primary fatty acid responsible for cardiovascular benefits
Fat-soluble, well absorbed in its native lipid matrix
Absorbed in the gastrointestinal tract; peppery throat sensation indicates presence and concentration
Rapidly absorbed with ~40-80% bioavailability; one of the most potent natural antioxidants (ORAC value)
Partially hydrolyzed to hydroxytyrosol in GI tract; bitter taste correlates with concentration
Compete with cholesterol for intestinal absorption, contributing to LDL-lowering effect

synergies.description: Used to dress vegetables on fasting days
Adding olive oil to carbohydrate meals slows glucose absorption
Olive oil recommended over omega-6-rich vegetable oils which promote inflammation
Olive oil should replace butter (saturated fat) and margarine (trans fats)
Both provide monounsaturated and polyunsaturated fats; PREDIMED trial tested both EVOO-supplemented and nut-supplemented Mediterranean diets with similar cardiovascular benefits
Both contain anti-inflammatory phytochemicals (oleocanthal in EVOO, ferulic acid in whole grains) that work through complementary pathways
EVOO enhances absorption of fat-soluble antioxidants (beta-carotene, vitamin E) from vegetables
Fat enables absorption of fat-soluble carotenoids
Fat enables absorption of fat-soluble vitamins K, A, E
Fat-soluble curcuminoids require dietary fat for absorption
